title: SQL throws TypeException if aggregations and limit statements are both present
poc: SELECT COUNT(*) AS val FROM test.simple_table ORDER BY val DESC;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS val FROM test.simple_table ORDER BY val DESC LIMIT 5;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS val FROM test.simple_table HAVING 1 > 0 ORDER BY val DESC LIMIT 5;
key_bug_features: Aggregations with limit statements cause TypeException
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test.agg_table("id" INTEGER, "value" DOUBLE PRECISION, "category" VARCHAR(50));
SELECT SUM(value) AS total_value FROM test.agg_table WHERE category = 'A' ORDER BY total_value DESC;
SELECT AVG(value) AS avg_value FROM test.agg_table GROUP BY category ORDER BY avg_value DESC LIMIT 2;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS count_value FROM test.agg_table HAVING COUNT(*) > 2 ORDER BY count_value DESC LIMIT 1;
